# Agent Machines

> Persistent AI agents as a one-click primitive. Deploy the combined unit: agent + home + skills + services + memory + scheduling + observation — on any container substrate.

## Summary

Agent Machines provisions a per-account Linux machine where everything persists to disk under `/home/machine/.agent-machines/`. The product is the **combined primitive** — not a bare container, not a framework-only layer, not a memory bolt-on.

Humans use the dashboard to provision, watch, and customize. The endgame is programmatic control: an MCP server and CLI so head agents spin up, route, observe, and tear down worker machines.

Open source (MIT): <https://github.com/Kevin-Liu-01/agent-machines>

## Core facts

- **Primitive**: persistent agent-on-a-machine with harness, not VM-only hosting.
- **Persistence**: real filesystem at `~/.agent-machines/` — chats, MEMORY.md, FTS5 sessions, cron, skills, MCP config, logs.
- **Agents**: Hermes, OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex CLI — native tool sets differ per runtime.
- **Providers**: Dedalus Machines, E2B Sandbox, Sprites.dev (interchangeable substrate).
- **Harness** (registry-driven — counts from `web/lib/platform/harness.ts` + loadout):
  - **161** SKILL.md skills
  - **27** ranked service routes (MCP → CLI → skills per vendor)
  - **35** MCP catalog servers (2 core · 28 bundled · 4 IDE · 1 reference)
  - **23+** closed-loop CLIs
  - **9–23** agent-native tools (Hermes 23 · OpenClaw 18 · Claude Code 11 · Codex 9)
  - **12** task-route categories
  - **113** trusted-addons registry entries (skills, MCPs, CLIs, plugins, sources)
- **Observation**: sessions, tool calls, skill invocations, logs, cost — orchestrate what you can see.
- **Programmatic surface (roadmap)**: Agent Machines MCP + CLI for agent-to-agent fleet orchestration.

## Important pages

- [Landing](https://www.agent-machines.dev/)
- [FAQ](https://www.agent-machines.dev/faq)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/Kevin-Liu-01/agent-machines)
- [Hermes agent runtime](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent) (optional runtime, not the product)
- [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw)
- [Dedalus Machines](https://docs.dedaluslabs.ai/dcs)

## Design

Reticle/Sigil UI. Service/task rankings mirror kevin-wiki `tool-hierarchy.mdc`.
